target.name: Boom Supersonic
target.founder: Blake Scholl, Joe Wilding, Josh Krall

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $1.5B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Supersonic & Hypersonic (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 1 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 1 same-sector peers exist in Supersonic & Hypersonic, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Boom Supersonic is a Series D stage company in the Supersonic & Hypersonic sector, currently valued at $1.5B post-money following $600M+ in total capital raised. The comparable universe is sparse: two public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and one private peer (Hermeus) operate in adjacent aerospace and defense niches, though none directly overlap on stage and sector simultaneously.

Given the narrow peer set, a Peer-Median approach was not applicable—fewer than three same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables exist within the Supersonic & Hypersonic category. Stage-Progression methodology was therefore the primary triangulation lens, applying a 1.5x step-up multiplier typical of Series D-to-later-stage progression to the $1.5B base valuation. This yields an implied forward valuation of $2.2B.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $1.9B to $2.6B, with a central estimate of $2.2B. This range reflects moderate uplift from the current round consistent with market stage dynamics, but carries material uncertainty given the absence of narrow-sector public or late-stage private comparables. Key risks to valuation include regulatory pathway clarity for supersonic certification, production timeline execution, and sustained funding availability in a capital-intensive, pre-revenue development phase. The single applicable method limits confidence; additional peer data or near-term revenue milestones would improve precision.
